name: feat-unify-messaging-contract-and-execution-model
title: feat: Unify messaging contract and execution model
description: Refine the messaging packages into one consistent execution model for direct publish, outbox publish, class subscriptions, and runtime subscriptions as a hard public-contract replacement while preserving deterministic defaults and telemetry parity.
verification_command: dotnet test --project tests/Headless.Messaging.Abstractions.Tests.Unit/Headless.Messaging.Abstractions.Tests.Unit.csproj --no-build && dotnet test --project tests/Headless.Messaging.Core.Tests.Unit/Headless.Messaging.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj && dotnet test --project tests/Headless.Messaging.OpenTelemetry.Tests.Unit/Headless.Messaging.OpenTelemetry.Tests.Unit.csproj && dotnet test --project tests/Headless.Messaging.RedisStreams.Tests.Unit/Headless.Messaging.RedisStreams.Tests.Unit.csproj

userStories.title: [M] Define unified abstractions and DI registration surface
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. Replace the current split registration experience with one explicit DI entrypoint and deterministic auto-registration rules for message handlers while keeping class handlers centered on IConsume<T>. This story assumes the legacy registration surface is removed rather than preserved as a parallel API.
acceptanceCriteria: A single DI registration surface exists for direct publish, outbox publish, handler scanning, and explicit overrides without requiring Build-style follow-up calls.
Auto-registration scans concrete closed IConsume<T> handlers, registers them once per closed interface, and fails startup on duplicate topic/group/handler identity conflicts.
Convention-based defaults for topic, group, and handler identity are deterministic, documented, and only overridden through explicit audited configuration.
The superseded public registration DSL is removed or folded into the new surface instead of being kept as a long-lived compatibility layer.
notes: Replaced the registration surface with AddMessaging, SubscribeFromAssembly*, Subscribe*, and convention-based deterministic defaults.
Focused verification passed through the messaging abstractions and core builder-related suites.

userStories.title: [M] Align direct and outbox publish execution
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. Converge direct and outbox publish flows on the same application-facing experience, default resolution path, and validation model while isolating delivery-mode differences to infrastructure internals. This story assumes the old public publisher split is replaced rather than retained beside the new contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Direct and outbox publishers expose the same primary publish experience, including shared options for headers, correlation, and convention-based topic resolution.
Mutable ambient publisher state such as transaction or service-provider mutation is removed from the primary app-facing DX or isolated behind scoped infrastructure.
Validation remains fail-fast by default, and all non-default behavior is explicit and emitted through logs and diagnostics.
The superseded public publisher surface does not remain available as a long-lived side-by-side API after the redesign ships.
notes: Introduced IMessagePublisher and aligned the primary direct and outbox publish API around shared PublishOptions and scoped outbox infrastructure.
The rejected PublishOptions.Delay experiment was reverted; delayed publishing intentionally remains a separate outbox API rather than part of the shared primary publish contract.

userStories.title: [L] Add runtime function subscriptions on the shared execution core
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. Introduce ephemeral runtime function subscriptions for broker-attached messages, backed by the same scoped DI, filters, retry, correlation, and diagnostics pipeline used by class handlers.
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime subscribe and unsubscribe support ephemeral broker-attached function handlers with scoped DI, filters, retry, correlation, and diagnostics parity with class handlers.
The runtime registry uses atomic snapshot updates so concurrent subscribe or unsubscribe operations cannot corrupt registration state or expose partially updated handlers.

Duplicate registration conflicts reject by default, in-flight unsubscribe semantics are defined and tested, and deterministic handler identity is required unless an explicit auditable opt-out is enabled.
notes: Runtime subscriptions now attach named function handlers through an atomic mutable registry and execute through the same scoped consume pipeline as class handlers.
Focused verification passed through runtime subscriber unit and integration coverage in the messaging core suite.

userStories.title: [M] Preserve telemetry, docs, and regression coverage
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. Preserve diagnostics and correlation parity, update public docs, and add regression coverage so the new contract does not silently break observability or consumer guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing diagnostic listener and metric names required by dashboards remain preserved or aliased so telemetry consumers do not silently break.
Correlation and message identity metadata remain consistent across direct publish, outbox publish, and runtime subscriptions.
Public docs and XML documentation are updated to reflect the new contract, defaults, opt-outs, and verification guidance.
notes: Scope corrected to messaging-only after reverting the prior out-of-scope edits from US-005.
Added a regression test that locks the public messaging diagnostic listener and metric contract names in place for dashboards and OpenTelemetry consumers.
Updated messaging README, llm guidance, and runtime subscription XML docs to document AddMessaging, runtime subscriptions, deterministic defaults, and fail-fast duplicate behavior.
Focused verification passed through the messaging OpenTelemetry suite and the narrowed messaging-only verification gate.

userStories.title: [S] Align consumer lifecycle contract with scoped dispatch semantics
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. Align the public lifecycle contract with the actual scoped dispatch model so consumer lifecycle hooks are deterministic, documented, and covered by explicit per-dispatch tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Public lifecycle documentation states that IConsumerLifecycle hooks run per delivery on the scoped consumer instance rather than once at application startup or shutdown.
Consumer lifecycle tests explicitly verify that OnStartingAsync and OnStoppingAsync run around each dispatch and preserve the documented failure semantics.
Messaging package guidance and llm docs reflect the same lifecycle contract so consumers are not steered toward incorrect app-wide startup assumptions.
notes: Aligned IConsumerLifecycle XML docs, package guidance, and llm guidance to the scoped per-dispatch execution model already enforced by the dispatcher.
Added explicit lifecycle regression coverage showing OnStartingAsync and OnStoppingAsync run around each dispatch with the documented failure semantics.

findings: ConsumerRegistry freezes on first read and throws on later register or update, which blocks runtime-safe ephemeral subscriptions (src/Headless.Messaging.Core/ConsumerRegistry.cs:17).

DirectPublisher and OutboxPublisher duplicate topic resolution, correlation header generation, and validation while exposing different application-facing ergonomics (src/Headless.Messaging.Core/Internal/DirectPublisher.cs:58, src/Headless.Messaging.Core/Internal/OutboxPublisher.cs:166).

CompiledMessageDispatcher currently invokes IConsumerLifecycle startup and shutdown hooks per message even though the public contract describes once-per-consumer-lifetime behavior (src/Headless.Messaging.Abstractions/IConsumerLifecycle.cs:85, src/Headless.Messaging.Core/Internal/CompiledMessageDispatcher.cs:131).

Internal package guidance already positions messaging as type-safe scan-based registration with outbox and direct publish modes, so the redesign should preserve that mental model while simplifying the surface (docs/llms/messaging.txt).

MassTransit consumer configuration emphasizes convention-based endpoint naming with explicit override points, which supports deterministic default registration without hiding opt-outs (https://masstransit.io/documentation/configuration/consumers).

MassTransit scoped middleware shows that publish and consume cross-cutting behavior should flow through scoped filters rather than separate ad-hoc hooks per execution path (https://masstransit.io/documentation/configuration/middleware/scoped).

MassTransit transactional outbox reinforces aligning direct and durable publish APIs while isolating reliability behavior behind infrastructure responsibilities (https://masstransit.io/documentation/patterns/transactional-outbox).

MassTransit mediator supports runtime connect and disconnect for handlers and consumers, which is a useful reference point for safe ephemeral runtime subscriptions (https://masstransit.io/documentation/concepts/mediator).

Foundatio.Mediator favors a minimal handler contract plus a shared middleware pipeline, reinforcing the choice to keep class handlers centered on one shape rather than fragmenting the model further (https://github.com/FoundatioFx/Foundatio.Mediator/blob/main/README.md).
